name: Victoria
tagline: Butchart Gardens, Mild Pacific Breezes, and Island Living North of the Border
description: Victoria sits at the southern tip of Vancouver Island, offering Canada's mildest climate, English-style gardens, and a relaxed island pace of life. With universal healthcare, a walkable inner harbor, thriving farm-to-table dining, and year-round outdoor recreation, Victoria delivers exceptional quality of life for retirees who want natural beauty without harsh winters. Housing is pricier than most Canadian cities but still well below Vancouver.

quickFacts.visa: No visa required for Canadian citizens. Others: Super Visa (for parents/grandparents, up to 5 years) or Visitor Record extensions. No dedicated retirement visa — Canada lacks a retiree-specific immigration pathway.
quickFacts.safetyRating: Very Safe — consistently ranked among Canada's safest cities
quickFacts.nearestAirport: Victoria International Airport (YYJ) — 30 min from downtown
quickFacts.climate: Mild maritime Mediterranean with warm, dry summers and cool, rainy winters. Rarely drops below freezing. Canada's mildest city.

housing.description: 1-bedroom apartment in Esquimalt or Saanich, away from the tourist harbor but well-connected by bus

dining.description: Fish and chips on the wharf, casual pub meals, and home cooking with local farm produce

healthcare.description: Universal BC MSP healthcare covers most costs; budget for dental, vision, and prescriptions

transportation.description: BC Transit monthly pass; Victoria is very bikeable with dedicated cycling infrastructure

entertainment.description: Beacon Hill Park walks, free harbor strolls, whale watching from shore, library and community events

1M.lifestyle: Victoria is workable at this budget, helped by universal BC MSP healthcare. A one-bedroom in Esquimalt or Saanich keeps costs manageable, and the city is bikeable with good cycling infrastructure. You will cook most meals at home using local farm produce, with occasional pub meals and fish and chips on the wharf. Victoria has Canada's mildest winters — more rain than snow — though housing costs are higher than many comparable Canadian cities.

housing.description: Spacious 2-bedroom apartment in James Bay or Fairfield, walking distance to the Inner Harbour

dining.description: Regular meals at farm-to-table restaurants, weekend brunches in Oak Bay, craft brewery scene

healthcare.description: Universal MSP coverage plus private supplemental for dental, vision, massage therapy, and faster specialist access

transportation.description: Reliable car for island exploration, BC Ferries trips to Vancouver, full insurance and transit pass

entertainment.description: Royal BC Museum membership, kayaking in the Gorge, whale watching tours, live music at Hermann's Jazz Club

2M.lifestyle: A two-bedroom in James Bay or Fairfield, walking distance to the Inner Harbour. Regular dining at farm-to-table restaurants, a car for island exploration and BC Ferries trips to Vancouver, and private supplemental health insurance for dental and vision. The mild climate supports year-round cycling and gardening. Victoria's dining scene is strong for a city its size, and the craft brewery scene adds variety. Universal healthcare keeps baseline medical costs minimal.

housing.description: 3-bedroom character home in Oak Bay or waterfront condo in the Inner Harbour with mountain views

dining.description: Fine dining at Brasserie L'Ecole and Stage Wine Bar, private winery tours in Cowichan Valley, seafood feasts

healthcare.description: Universal coverage plus comprehensive private insurance with executive health screening and wellness services

transportation.description: Late-model vehicle, premium insurance, regular BC Ferries trips, occasional floatplane to Vancouver

entertainment.description: Victoria Golf Club membership, sailing in the Strait of Juan de Fuca, Pacific Opera season tickets, art gallery patronage

3M.lifestyle: A character home in Oak Bay or a waterfront condo in the Inner Harbour with mountain views. Fine dining at Brasserie L'Ecole and Stage Wine Bar, golf club membership, and regular floatplane commutes to Vancouver. Winery tours in the Cowichan Valley and sailing in the Strait of Juan de Fuca are easy weekend activities. Comprehensive private health insurance and a good vehicle for island living round out a comfortable lifestyle with solid margin.

housing.description: Luxury waterfront home in Cordova Bay or estate property in the Uplands with ocean and mountain views

dining.description: Private chef for weekly dinner parties, fine dining as routine, curated BC wine collection, farm-direct sourcing

healthcare.description: Universal coverage plus executive health programs, naturopathic medicine, premium private supplements

transportation.description: Luxury SUV, second vehicle, Harbour Air floatplane commutes, BC Ferries comfort class

entertainment.description: Royal Victoria Yacht Club membership, private sailing, art collecting from local galleries, symphony patronage

domesticHelp.description: Weekly house cleaning, gardener for estate grounds, seasonal landscaping, property maintenance

luxuryExtras.description: Cottage on Salt Spring Island, quarterly international travel, boat moorage, philanthropic memberships

5M.lifestyle: A waterfront home in Cordova Bay or an estate in the Uplands with ocean and mountain views, Royal Victoria Yacht Club membership, a housekeeper, and a cottage on Salt Spring Island or another Gulf Island. Regular floatplane trips to Vancouver and business class for longer flights. Universal healthcare keeps medical costs minimal. Victoria is a small city, so the dining and cultural scene is limited compared to Vancouver, but the coastal setting and mild climate are the main draws.

housing.description: Grand estate in the Uplands or waterfront compound in North Saanich with private dock and gardens

dining.description: Full-time personal chef, exclusive supper club events, private winery dinners, catered garden parties

healthcare.description: Universal coverage plus concierge medicine, executive wellness team, international medical coverage

transportation.description: Multiple luxury vehicles, private floatplane charter, yacht, first-class international travel

entertainment.description: Major arts philanthropy, private sailing regattas, international travel, exclusive golf and yacht memberships

domesticHelp.description: Full estate staff: housekeeper, chef, gardener, property manager, personal assistant

luxuryExtras.description: Multiple Gulf Island properties, private yacht, art patronage, charitable foundation, bespoke travel experiences

10M.lifestyle: A grand estate in the Uplands or a waterfront compound in North Saanich with private dock and English gardens, a cook who comes in several times a week, regular housekeeping, and first-class international travel. A boat for Gulf Island exploration and comprehensive healthcare on top of universal coverage. Victoria is a small city of about 400,000 people in the metro area, so the luxury services and fine dining options are limited. At this budget, most of the spending goes to travel, property, and the quiet comfort of island living.
